title: Signed comparison fails for sizes not multiple of 8, or when reference value is read from a file
body:
Please regard the following code
t.sv:
```systemverilog
module t;

  parameter W=17;
  
  wire signed [W-1:0] op;
  logic signed [W-1:0]  opref;
  int fd;

  assign op = 17'h1ffff;

  initial begin
    fd=$fopen("t.txt", "r");
    #1 $fscanf(fd, "%d", opref);
    if(op !== opref) $display("Mismatch: op=%b, opref=%b",op,opref);
    else $display("pass");
    $fclose(fd);
    $stop;
  end

endmodule: t
``` 
t.txt:
```
-1
```

I run: 
``` shell
verilator --binary t.sv ; ./obj_dir/Vt 
```
I get this simulation output:
```
Mismatch: op=11111111111111111, opref=11111111111111111
```
ie. The comparison failed, although both signals are bit equivalent !
Interestingly, this mismatch didn't occur if I set bit-width (W parameter) to 8 or 16.
Also, this mismatch does not happen if I set opref to -1 in the SV code, ie: if I replace the $fscanf line with:
```systemverilog
#1 opref=-1;
```
What 'verilator --version' are you using? 
Verilator 5.006 2023-01-22 rev (Debian 5.006-3)

Did you try it with the git master version?
No

What OS and distribution are you using?
Debian bookworm on arm64

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Maybe
